question_text: Let a = 2i - j + 2k and b = i + 2j - k. Let a vector v be in the plane containing a and b. If v is perpendicular to the vector 3i + 2j - k and its projection on a is 19 units, then |2v|^2 is equal to ____.
question_text_latex: Let $\vec a=2\hat i-\hat j+2\hat k$ and $\vec b=\hat i+2\hat j-\hat k$. Let a vector $\vec v$ be in the plane containing $\vec a$ and $\vec b$. If $\vec v$ is perpendicular to the vector $3\hat i+2\hat j-\hat k$ and its projection on $\vec a$ is $19$ units, then $|2\vec v|^2$ is equal to $\underline{\qquad}$.
subject: Mathematics
solution_explanation:
Let v = p a + q b.
Then v . (3i + 2j - k) = 0 gives
p(a . c) + q(b . c) = 0, where c = 3i + 2j - k.
Since a . c = 2 and b . c = 8, we get p + 4q = 0.
Also, the projection of v on a is 19 units, so (v . a)/|a| = 19.
Here |a| = 3 and v . a = 9p + 3q, hence 9p + 3q = 57, or 3p + q = 19.
Solving p + 4q = 0 and 3p + q = 19 gives p = 6 and q = -3/2.
Therefore v = 6a - (3/2)b = (21/2, -9, 27/2).
So |v|^2 = 747/2 and hence |2v|^2 = 4|v|^2 = 1494.

manual_solution_latex:
Let $\vec v=p\vec a+q\vec b$.
With $\vec c=3\hat i+2\hat j-\hat k$, the condition $\vec v\perp \vec c$ gives
\[\vec v\cdot \vec c=p(\vec a\cdot \vec c)+q(\vec b\cdot \vec c)=0.\]
Since $\vec a\cdot \vec c=2$ and $\vec b\cdot \vec c=8$, we get
\[p+4q=0.\]
The projection of $\vec v$ on $\vec a$ is $19$, so
\[\frac{\vec v\cdot \vec a}{|\vec a|}=19.\]
Now $|\vec a|=3$ and $\vec v\cdot \vec a=9p+3q$, hence
\[9p+3q=57\iff 3p+q=19.\]
Solving the two linear equations gives $p=6$ and $q=-\frac32$.
Thus
\[\vec v=6\vec a-\frac32\vec b=\left(\frac{21}{2},-9,\frac{27}{2}\right).\]
Therefore
\[|\vec v|^2=\frac{747}{2}\quad\Rightarrow\quad |2\vec v|^2=4|\vec v|^2=1494.\]

question_text: Let v = alpha i + 2j - 3k, w = 2 alpha i + j - k, and u be a vector such that |u| = alpha > 0. If the minimum value of the scalar triple product u . (v x w) is -alpha sqrt(3401), and (u . i)^2 = m/n where m and n are coprime natural numbers, then m + n is equal to ____.
question_text_latex: Let $\vec v=\alpha\hat i+2\hat j-3\hat k$, $\vec w=2\alpha\hat i+\hat j-\hat k$, and $\vec u$ be a vector such that $|\vec u|=\alpha>0$. If the minimum value of the scalar triple product $\vec u\cdot(\vec v\times\vec w)$ is $-\alpha\sqrt{3401}$, and $(\vec u\cdot\hat i)^2=\dfrac{m}{n}$ where $m$ and $n$ are coprime natural numbers, then $m+n$ is equal to $\underline{\qquad}$.
subject: Mathematics
solution_explanation:
We have v x w = i - 5 alpha j - 3 alpha k.
Therefore |v x w|^2 = 1 + 34 alpha^2.
For fixed |u| = alpha, the minimum possible value of u . (v x w) is -|u| |v x w| = -alpha |v x w|.
This is given as -alpha sqrt(3401), so |v x w| = sqrt(3401).
Hence 1 + 34 alpha^2 = 3401, which gives alpha^2 = 100 and alpha = 10.
The minimum occurs when u is opposite to v x w, so u = -alpha (v x w)/|v x w|.
Thus u . i = -alpha / sqrt(3401), and therefore (u . i)^2 = 100/3401.
So m = 100, n = 3401, and m + n = 3501.

manual_solution_latex:
First compute
\[\vec v\times\vec w=
\begin{vmatrix}
\hat i & \hat j & \hat k\\
\alpha & 2 & -3\\
2\alpha & 1 & -1
\end{vmatrix}
=\hat i-5\alpha\hat j-3\alpha\hat k.\]
Hence
\[|\vec v\times\vec w|^2=1+25\alpha^2+9\alpha^2=1+34\alpha^2.\]
For fixed $|\vec u|=\alpha$, the minimum of $\vec u\cdot(\vec v\times\vec w)$ is
\[-|\vec u|\,|\vec v\times\vec w|=-\alpha|\vec v\times\vec w|.\]
This equals $-\alpha\sqrt{3401}$, so
\[|\vec v\times\vec w|=\sqrt{3401}\Rightarrow 1+34\alpha^2=3401.\]
Therefore $\alpha^2=100$, hence $\alpha=10$.
At the minimum, $\vec u$ is opposite to $\vec v\times\vec w$, so
\[\vec u=-\alpha\frac{\vec v\times\vec w}{|\vec v\times\vec w|}.\]
Therefore
\[\vec u\cdot\hat i=-\frac{\alpha}{\sqrt{3401}},\qquad (\vec u\cdot\hat i)^2=\frac{100}{3401}.\]
Thus $m=100$, $n=3401$, and
\[m+n=3501.\]
